$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `project_doc_mount_links` repo — a
mount-index sibling-DB repo of Phase 2, a near-clone of the pilot
`group_character_members`. One committed file drives BOTH sides of the
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness project_doc_mount_links tier-2 test).
SIBLING DB: in v4 this table lives in quilltap-mount-index.db (NOT the main
DB). The builder/oracle point SQLITE_MOUNT_INDEX_PATH at the fixture and read
it back through getRawMountIndexDatabase(); the Rust Writer simply opens that
file by path. This corpus banks no new marshaling shape — the table is the
plainest join: id + two required UUID-as-TEXT columns (projectId,
mountPointId, both cross-DB refs stored as plain TEXT, no FK constraint) +
the two timestamps. projectId references projects.id in the MAIN db.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one every other
tier-2 fixture uses. It is NOT the real Friday pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

ops.comment: Link the same project to a third mount point.
A second project linked to an existing mount point.
Re-point seed row 1 to a different mount point. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.
Remove the second-project link created above.
